operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC 2015 - Mentors
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC 2015 - Mentors
Messages: 3
Date range: 27 Apr 2015 to 2015-04-28
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14cfc686436d8ac5

============================================================

--- Message 1/3 ---
From: Arley Ristar
Date: 27 Apr 2015

Congratulations to all approved! Some questions: 1. How do we know who are the mentors for each accepted project? 2. Should we be discussing the Community Bonding Period here or in private with the mentor?

--- Message 2/3 ---
From: Gavriel Loria
Date: 27 Apr 2015

The accepted students page says who your mentor(s) should be: http://www.google-melange.com/gsoc/projects/list/google/gsoc2015
It looks like yours is mike duncan.

--- Message 3/3 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-04-28

Yi-Shan,

For the Minecraft projects that were accepted, the mentor is Lake Watkins, <nemquae@gmail.com>

It's generally better to keep discussions on this email list, or on the OpenCog Slack team which is about to be launched. Because for most projects there are many OpenCoggers besides the mentor who may have useful feedback.

-- Ben

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC 2015 - Natural Language Processing.
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC 2015 - Natural Language Processing.
Messages: 3
Date range: 15 Mar 2015 to 2015-03-15
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c1d37b1c2daa8d

============================================================

--- Message 1/3 ---
From: Ashish Bedi
Date: 15 Mar 2015

Sir, This is Ashish Kumar Bedi, a 2nd year undergraduate CS student at IIT Mandi, India. I am interested in contributing to opencog in NLP, specifically implementing simplified Word grammar parsing in Atomspace.

--- Message 2/3 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-16

Hi Ashish,

Given your math background, I wonder if you might consider this project:
http://wiki.opencog.org/w/GSoC_2015_Project_Ideas#Implement_and_integrate_Imprecise_Probability_truth_value_math

Basically this involves some Monte Carlo integration coding, and would entail you learning about probabilistic logic and imprecise probabilities.

I'm just thinking you may not have the right linguistics background for the Word Grammar project.

-- Ben

--- Message 3/3 ---
From: Ashish Bedi
Date: 2015-03-15

Sir, This is Ashish Kumar Bedi. I am interested in contributing to opencog in NLP project and specificaly in implementing simplified Word grammar parsing in Atomspace.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSOC 2015 (14b54ed3f2c399a9)
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSOC 2015
Messages: 7
Date range: 2015-02-04 to 2015-02-07
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14b54ed3f2c399a9

============================================================

--- Message 1/7 ---
From: sonawane.abhishek12@gmail.com
Date: 2015-02-04

I am a final year CS student interested in Machine Learning and NLP. I am interested in the Word Grammar Parsing project. What kind of background would be required?

--- Message 2/7 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-02-04

Yes, possibly. There are two things that make this difficult: this requires modification of the internals of link-grammar, and actually, I do not really understand the project. Ben, can you explain how the Word Grammar thing would work?

--- Message 3/7 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-02-04

Yeah, I'll answer this thread early next week at latest. I'm having Internet issues here in Ethiopia so am being sluggish answering emails...

--- Message 4/7 ---
From: sonawane.abhishek12@gmail.com
Date: 2015-02-05

What about Statistically similar expressions for question answering? Can I work on it?

--- Message 5/7 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-02-05

I think that would be a fine GSoC project. Regarding Word Grammar, I think the initial project is to implement a Word Grammar parser, since none exist.... My idea is to implement a Word Grammar parser utilizing the link grammar dictionary, but the Word Grammar parsing algorithm....

--- Message 6/7 ---
From: Abhishek Sonawane
Date: 2015-02-07

How would I go about Statistically similar expressions for question answering? What background would I need? I also liked the idea of Natural Language Generation.

--- Message 7/7 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-02-07

We are using several related approaches to finding statistically similar expressions. The simplest way to explain is by Dekang Lin's DIRT paper for finding synonyms. It is generalized by Poon and Domings Unsupervised language learning. I would like to see the second one replicated in opencog. With a minor twist, I think it could be used for basic question answering.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC 2015 application of intelligent agents to Human-Computer Interaction
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC 2015 application of intelligent agents to Human-Computer Interaction
Messages: 4
Date range: 11 Mar 2015 to 12 Mar 2015
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c0cbcdf6661126

============================================================

--- Message 1/4 ---
From: Kirill Mishchenko
Date: 11 Mar 2015

Hello, My name is Kirill. I'm a PhD student in computer science. I'm interested in contributing to the clustering and microplanning tasks. Can you give comments on the microplanning task?

--- Message 2/4 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-12

Hey William, If we were to have a GSoC student working on microplanning, what would you suggest they focus on?

ben

--- Message 3/4 ---
From: William Ma
Date: 12 Mar 2015

That's a difficult question. Options include: 1. Remove SuReal dependency, 2. Port to C++/Python, 3. Remove Sentence Form rules file usage, 5. Rewrite the whole thing with completely new algorithm. The microplanning code is at https://github.com/opencog/opencog/tree/master/opencog/nlp/microplanning

--- Message 4/4 ---
From: Kirill Mishchenko
Date: 12 Mar 2015

Ok. Thanks for the ideas. I will investigate them.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSOC_2015_FLAG -- Haskell Bindings for OpenCog
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSOC_2015_FLAG -- Haskell Bindings for OpenCog
Messages: 5
Date range: 2015-03-03 to 2015-03-09
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14be8c366052ec1d

============================================================

--- Message 1/5 ---
From: Kaivan Shah
Date: 2015-03-03

I am a sophomore at Texas A&M University, interested in the Haskell bindings project. My competency in C++ and Haskell is intermediate.

--- Message 2/5 ---
From: Nil Geisweiller
Date: 2015-03-05

You would need to study the AtomSpace and its API, drawing inspiration from existing binding languages like Scheme. You need to demonstrate in your proposal that you understand the task and sketch a step by step plan. It looks like I would be the main mentor, but Ben and Linas would help as well.

--- Message 3/5 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-08

I have looked over your example Haskell code, and it is reasonably clean and elegant. My sense is that making Haskell bindings to OpenCog might end up being a bit much for you. I think it might be a better fit to make a prototype exploring Haskell-based evolutionary learning of complex programmatic constructs using fold and GenProg, which could be inserted into MOSES as an alternative for what happens inside a deme.

--- Message 4/5 ---
From: Nil Geisweiller
Date: 2015-03-09

A full binding is probably too big indeed, but we could aim at a simplified binding, it would still be useful. Ben, if you think you can handle most of the mentoring for the GenProg project, I am happy to help for the MOSES binding part.

--- Message 5/5 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-09

I can personally mentor a GenProg project, but probably not a Haskell-bindings project. I think you would be better for the latter.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSOC 2015 NLP
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSOC 2015 NLP
Messages: 6
Date range: 2015-03-03 to 2015-03-07
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14be8c4730893b97

============================================================

--- Message 1/6 ---
From: Darshan agarwal
Date: 2015-03-03

I am a 3rd year CS student interested in NLP. I am interested in the Reference Resolution project. Can I know which projects in NLP can be opted for GSoC 2015?

--- Message 2/6 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-05

See the shiny new up to date GSoC 2015 Ideas page.

--- Message 3/6 ---
From: Darshan agarwal
Date: 2015-03-07

I want to contribute on the project Unsupervised Language Learning. I am particularly interested in Morphology and Disjunct extraction, parse ranking.

--- Message 4/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-03-07

What languages do you know well enough to read and write? Well enough to understand their morphology?

--- Message 5/6 ---
From: Darshan agarwal
Date: 2015-03-08

I know English and Hindi well enough.

--- Message 6/6 ---
From: Linas Vepstas
Date: 2015-03-07

We have someone else working on the syntax-learning pieces, but no one working on morphology learning. English morphology is not really worth pursuing. In order to determine if the morphology work is going well, you would have to actually look at it, and see if what it is generating matches what it should be.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC 2015 Performance Measurement Suite
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC 2015 Performance Measurement Suite
Messages: 3
Date range: 17 Mar 2015 to 18 Mar 2015
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c295e8fe3ba139

============================================================

--- Message 1/3 ---
From: Tim Schwab
Date: 17 Mar 2015

Hello, my name is Tim Schwab. I've decided to write my GSoC proposal on an overarching performance measurement suite of OpenCog, an extension of the AtomSpace benchmark by Joel Pitt.

--- Message 2/3 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-18

Tim, The proposal looks OK, though it's pretty high level. Can you point to any other specific performance measurement suites out there? Also, if you have time to submit some patch to the OpenCog codebase, that would increase your odds of acceptance.

thanks
Ben

--- Message 3/3 ---
From: tschwab@g.clemson.edu
Date: 18 Mar 2015

Thanks for the tips, I was a bit worried its too high level. I'll try to refine it a bit more.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC 2015 Proposal for PLN Inference on extracted semantic relationships
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC 2015 Proposal for PLN Inference on extracted semantic relationships
Messages: 6
Date range: 2015-03-23 to 2015-03-27
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c436ee2ce48b17

============================================================

--- Message 1/6 ---
From: Prateek Saxena
Date: 2015-03-23

Thanks for all your guidance in introducing me to opencog. I have begun writing my GSoC 2015 application for PLN inference on extracted semantic relationships. I need more information about specific tasks and expected timelines.

--- Message 2/6 ---
From: Prateek Saxena
Date: 2015-03-25

I have been working on my proposal and am close to getting it finished. The sections I am working on are regarding specific tasks and timelines.

--- Message 3/6 ---
From: Amen Belayneh
Date: 2015-03-25

The main tasks will involve porting the different rules and examples associated with them. It is hard to get a timeline for individual tasks. Make it up as you judge fit. Just submit your proposal on GSoC site and let us know here.

--- Message 4/6 ---
From: Prateek Saxena
Date: 2015-03-26

I have submitted the first draft of my proposal on the GSoC website. Could you have a look at it?

--- Message 5/6 ---
From: Amen Belayneh
Date: 2015-03-27

Your proposal is clear, nothing much to be added, as far as I can tell. Good Luck.

--- Message 6/6 ---
From: Prateek Saxena
Date: 2015-03-27

Thank you for getting back so quickly.

operations.name: RawData+opencog-ml+GSoC Clustering Idea; What to implement
operations.content:
OpenCog Mailing List Thread: GSoC Clustering Idea; What to implement
Messages: 3
Date range: 19 Mar 2015 to 2015-03-20
Source: opencog@googlegroups.com
Thread ID: 14c33855b070de27

============================================================

--- Message 1/3 ---
From: Islam Faisal
Date: 19 Mar 2015

Hello, I am an undergraduate student in computer science and mathematics. I found this clustering http://wiki.opencog.org/w/Clustering project and am really interested to work on it during the upcoming summer.

--- Message 2/3 ---
From: Aaron Nitzkin
Date: 2015-03-20

I've been experimenting with the clustering outside the atomspace, using Python, and we are learning some important things. I believe the real work will be done in C++. Linas currently has a GSoC mentee working on atomspace data representations for the clustering.

--- Message 3/3 ---
From: Ben Goertzel
Date: 2015-03-20

We haven't made any progress on implementing clustering in the Atomspace, but definitely would like to see this done! It could be done in C++, python or Scheme, actually

-- Ben
